payload.answer: Propose a conservative, atomized memory plan: establish a small core card set with indices and a lazy-per-row-card mechanism; defer full per-row instantiation until explicit need arises.
payload.decision: selective_atomization: choose a single durable, non-redundant memory card approach from the proposed 500 CSV rows, preserving ambiguity and avoiding superseded data.

entity_resolution.reason: No clear mapping from 500 raw rows to durable, meaningful memory cards without user-defined criteria
Ambiguity about which rows are essential and how to maintain durability among 500 entries

memory_cards.statement: Adopt a strategy to atomize the 500 CSV rows into a compact set of memorizable cards, but do not commit to materializing all 500 until requirements are clarified.

entity_resolution.reason: Maintains durability while avoiding premature commitment to all rows

memory_cards.statement: Use a two-tier representation: (1) a minimal index of 10-20 core cards capturing cross-cutting context, (2) per-row cards only when requested or when a row becomes contextually needed.

details: Selected high-level atomization approach instead of materializing all 500 cards immediately.
Outlined two-tier strategy to balance durability and memory footprint.

repair_options: Provide criteria for selecting core cards (e.g., highest coverage of topics, most referenced rows).
Define trigger events that would instantiate per-row cards (e.g., user query matches a row).
Specify durable IDs and versioning to prevent superseded data from appearing as current.
Implement a review cadence to retire or merge cards when duplicates or outdated information emerge.
